mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in payroll employment growth above 150k per month signals strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in high-payroll growth periods fall below 0.5% in > 40% of cases, or the signal's IC drops below 0.05 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in both GDP growth and housing starts signals weak aggregate demand, prompting central bank intervention that generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in demand contraction scenarios show negative exposure to the combined GDPC1-HOUST signal (beta < -0.2 in rolling 3-year regressions).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% annualized triggers hawkish central bank rhetoric that increases equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high-inflation periods exceed 1.5% in > 50% of cases, or the signal's IC falls below -0.05 in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in industrial production signals manufacturing sector distress that reduces corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in industrial contraction scenarios show positive exposure to the INDPRO signal (beta > 0.15 in rolling 3-year regressions).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a recessionary demand shock that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in demand-collapse scenarios show positive exposure to the RSAFS-UNRATE interaction (beta > 0.2 in rolling 3-year regressions).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in unemployment and wage growth signals a labor market overheating that prompts rate hikes, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in labor-market overheating scenarios exceed 1.0% in > 40% of cases, or the signal's IC drops below -0.03 in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production combined with stable inflation indicates strong supply-side dynamics that support positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in supply-side expansion scenarios show negative exposure to the INDPRO-CPIAUCSL interaction (beta < -0.15 in rolling 3-year regressions).
